surprise: The Bergman model's homeostatic feedback makes random insulin delivery surprisingly effective on basal-only tasks (76.7% TIR), but PPO outperforms random on BolusAdvisor (1324.4 vs 568.4) and ClosedLoop (1868.6 vs -825.8).
mechanism: Endogenous insulin secretion in the Bergman model compensates for suboptimal exogenous delivery on simple basal tasks, but this compensation is insufficient for meal bolus timing and multi-objective constraints with IOB penalties.
implication: Multi-objective glucose management environments with safety constraints produce the strongest separation between learned and naive policies, establishing a gradient of benchmark difficulty.
baseline_margin: PPO achieves 1868.6 vs random -825.8 on ClosedLoop-v0, and 1324.4 vs 568.4 on BolusAdvisor-v0. BasalControl shows minimal separation (780.4 vs 913.0).
key_finding: Benchmark difficulty gradient: basal-only environments show least separation between random and learned policies, bolus environments show moderate separation, and full closed-loop with safety constraints shows the strongest signal.
